mappings.rationaleAI: Colonial America addresses the Americas (A), Spanish colonies (B), and English colonial development (C.3).
Spanish conquest addresses discovery and conquest (B.1).
Aztec civilization connects to conquest of Mexico (B.1).
Maya civilization connects to conquest of Central America (B.1).
The Revolution connects to the expulsion of French power (C.3.d).
The Founders connect to colonial achievements (C.3.c).
Colonial empires connect to Spanish/Portuguese (B) and English settlements (C).
British administration connects to English colonies (C.3) and mercantilism (C.3.b.i).
Slavery connects to Spanish colonial policy (B.2.b) and English colonial immigration (C.3.b.iii).
Colonial literature connects to the Spanish colonial empire (B).
Indigenous peoples connect to the Americas (A) and French-Indian conflicts (C.3.d).
Exploration connects to discovery (B.1), Norse voyages (C.1), and English exploration (C.2).
Capitalism connects to colonial economy (B.2.c).
Puritanism connects to religious settlement (C.3.a) and the Great Awakening (C.3.c).

Antislavery connects to colonial slavery (B.2.b).
Navigation connects to voyages of discovery (B.1, C.1).
Religious diversity connects to settlement (C.3.a) and the Great Awakening (C.3.c).
Immigration connects to colonial immigration (C.3.b.iii).
Economic development connects to colonial economy (B.2.c).
Viking Atlantic crossings connect to Norse voyages (C.1).
Migration connects to colonial immigration (C.3.b.iii).
Colonial exploitation connects to Spanish colonial policy (B).

Covers Canadian history from early European settlement including colonial foundations — directly relevant to the settlement of New France, missionaries, Indian relations, and the fur trade (unresolved leaf D.1).
